steps.title: Use the function values as points
steps.content: The values $h(0)=41$ and $h(1)=40$ are points on the graph: $(0,41)$ and $(1,40)$. This is linear, so two points are enough to fit the entire function.

steps.title: Run a linear regression
steps.content: In Desmos, type $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. The regression gives $m=-1$ and $b=41$, so the function is $$h(x)=-x+41.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is $h(x)=-x+41$, which gives $h(0)=41$ and $h(1)=40$. The other choices fail at least one of those two point checks. Choice A is correct.
